agentic_gap: Existing harnesses can run evaluations, but agent-specific alignment needs normalized trajectory records across prompts, tools, state updates, approvals, delegation messages, and final answers.
factory_implication: Reuse Inspect as execution/log substrate; define a factory trace schema that can be exported from Inspect logs and compared across providers.
evidence_note: Inspect documents tasks, solvers, scorers, logs, log viewer, dataframes, tools, agents, sandboxing, and tracing. garak orchestrates scans through probes/generators/detectors/harnesses/reports. HarmBench/JailbreakBench standardize red-team evaluations but are centered on attack/response outcomes.

agentic_gap: Probe catalogs focus on eliciting unsafe model outputs more than measuring multi-step agent governance failures in benign workflows.
factory_implication: Treat probe libraries and harm taxonomies as metadata inputs, not the main generator; build agentic scenario templates around safe analogues.
evidence_note: garak is explicitly organized around vulnerability probes and detectors; HarmBench/JailbreakBench curate attack behaviors and jailbreak artifacts; Inspect can host custom tasks but does not itself define this domain taxonomy.

agentic_gap: Agent tests need a threat model for authority boundaries, state access, delegation, and oversight, not only attacker prompt constraints.
factory_implication: Add per-task threat-model fields: allowed tools, restricted records, approval rules, untrusted context, escalation expectations, and non-goals.
evidence_note: HarmBench and JailbreakBench emphasize standardized red-team/jailbreak evaluation; JailbreakBench explicitly specifies threat models, system prompts, templates, and scoring functions. Inspect provides flexible mechanics but leaves threat modeling to the eval author.

agentic_gap: Static benchmark metadata rarely captures trace assertions, benign analogue mapping, false-positive risk, or developer remediation hints.
factory_implication: Define a minimal task spec with safety property, toy-domain analogue, required trace events, deterministic predicates, and failure labels.
evidence_note: JailbreakBench packages artifacts and leaderboard metadata; HarmBench standardizes behavior sets; Inspect datasets support metadata but no alignment-test-factory schema is imposed.

agentic_gap: Tool availability alone does not test whether the agent selects minimal-authority tools, avoids overreach, and records why a tool was used.
factory_implication: Build toy tools with inspectable side effects and deterministic assertions over tool names, arguments, counts, and state deltas.
evidence_note: Inspect supports custom/MCP/standard tools and tool calling in agent evaluations. garak, HarmBench, and JailbreakBench mostly target model text behavior rather than tool-mediated workflows.

agentic_gap: Stateful failures can occur when an agent silently trusts stale memory, overwrites context, or fails to distinguish task facts from misleading notes.
factory_implication: Create synthetic state stores and memory fixtures with explicit provenance and expected read/write behavior.
evidence_note: Inspect agents can use planning, memory, and tools, and logs can expose events, but reusable alignment memory scenarios and predicates are not supplied by the benchmark anchors.

agentic_gap: Delegated subtasks can drift from the original policy or transmit restricted context even when the lead agent final answer looks fine.
factory_implication: Model delegation as trace events and require deterministic checks on delegated instructions, inputs, outputs, and policy inheritance.
evidence_note: Inspect documents multi-agent primitives and external-agent bridges; the red-team/jailbreak anchors do not directly target delegation semantics.

agentic_gap: Approval mechanisms exist, but benchmarks need scenarios that test when agents ask, skip, over-request, or ignore denials.
factory_implication: Add policy fixtures with approval tokens/placeholders and trace assertions for required request, denial handling, and no unauthorized side effects.
evidence_note: Inspect supports tool approval and sandboxing; garak can scan security vulnerabilities but is not centered on permission workflow tests.

agentic_gap: One-turn refusal scoring misses whether an agent escalates when evidence is incomplete, conflicting, or authority is ambiguous.
factory_implication: Generate tasks with controlled ambiguity and deterministic expected escalation markers, abstentions, or clarification requests.
evidence_note: Inspect scorers can grade outputs; JailbreakBench includes over-refusal/refusal judging in its benchmark scope, but neither supplies agent workflow escalation fixtures.

agentic_gap: Final-answer scoring can pass an agent that reached the answer through impermissible reads, hidden assumptions, or unverified tool results.
factory_implication: Add violation predicates over trajectory T: observations, tool calls, state updates, approvals, delegation, and final response.
evidence_note: Inspect logs and dataframes expose evaluation internals, and garak detectors evaluate probe results, but reusable trace-level alignment assertions for agent trajectories are the factory gap.

agentic_gap: Existing deterministic scoring is strongest for final outputs; agentic tests need deterministic checks on intermediate side effects and compliance traces.
factory_implication: Prefer file/state/tool-call predicates first; reserve model judges for semantic residue and record why they were needed.
evidence_note: Inspect supports custom scorers and text comparisons; garak detectors and benchmark judges vary by probe and task; HarmBench/JailbreakBench include standardized scoring but much of the domain is response-centered.

agentic_gap: Benchmarks should preserve the alignment structure of harmful or sensitive settings without shipping operational abuse content.
factory_implication: Build generators for benign analogues such as synthetic customer files, restricted records, approval tokens, and toy operations systems.
evidence_note: The anchors provide datasets/probes/artifacts or harnesses, but not a reusable safe scenario generator for tool/memory/delegation alignment failures.

agentic_gap: Leaderboards and logs help reproducibility, but agent developers also need failure labels, trace snippets, and remediation-oriented summaries.
factory_implication: Package each run with task spec version, trace predicates, deterministic verdicts, optional judge rationale, and failure taxonomy labels.
evidence_note: Inspect has logs, log viewer, eval sets, and dataframes; JailbreakBench provides leaderboard-style comparison; HarmBench supports standardized comparisons; garak reports scan results.
